name: Frontend interview prep (Nagarro)
description: Frontend developer topics from the Nagarro/Tesco Hungary interview prep doc: JS internals, React, browser APIs, CSS, a11y, security and performance.

topics.name: Object vs Map
topics.description: Choosing between plain objects and Map for keyed collections; key coercion, iteration, size.

topics.name: Event delegation
topics.description: Capturing vs bubbling, delegating handlers to a common ancestor.

topics.name: WebSocket vs SSE
topics.description: Bidirectional WebSocket vs one-way server-sent events vs polling for realtime UI.

topics.name: CSS box-sizing
topics.description: content-box vs border-box and how padding/border affect the box model.

topics.name: Web accessibility (a11y)
topics.description: Semantic HTML, ARIA, keyboard navigation, focus management, contrast.

topics.name: Core Web Vitals
topics.description: LCP, CLS, INP and how to measure and improve perceived performance.

topics.name: JavaScript event loop
topics.description: Call stack, task queue, microtasks, and async execution order.

questions.title: Object vs Map
questions.prompt: When would you use a Map instead of a plain object in JavaScript, and what are the differences?
questions.answerStructureHint: Key types, key coercion, iteration order and size, and when each fits.

expectedPoints.label: Map keys can be any type
expectedPoints.description: Object keys are coerced to strings/symbols; Map keys can be objects, functions or any value.

expectedPoints.label: Map preserves insertion order and exposes size
expectedPoints.description: Map iterates in insertion order and has a size property; objects need Object.keys and have no direct size.

expectedPoints.label: Knows when to prefer each
expectedPoints.description: Map for frequent add/remove and non-string keys; object for fixed-shape records and JSON.

followUps.prompt: What problem do WeakMap and WeakSet solve that Map and Set do not?

questions.title: Event delegation
questions.prompt: What is event delegation and why is it useful? Explain capturing vs bubbling.
questions.answerStructureHint: Bubbling vs capturing phases, attaching one listener on an ancestor, and the benefits.

expectedPoints.label: Explains bubbling and capturing
expectedPoints.description: Events propagate down (capture) then up (bubble) through ancestors.

expectedPoints.label: One listener on a common ancestor
expectedPoints.description: Attach a single handler on a parent instead of one per child.

expectedPoints.label: Names the benefits
expectedPoints.description: Works for dynamically added elements and reduces memory/listener count.

followUps.prompt: How do stopPropagation and preventDefault differ?

questions.title: WebSocket vs SSE for live UI
questions.prompt: You need live updates in a web app. How would you choose between WebSocket, server-sent events and polling?
questions.answerStructureHint: Direction (one-way vs two-way), frequency, reconnection, and infrastructure fit.

expectedPoints.label: Frames around the requirement
expectedPoints.description: Anchors the choice in direction and update frequency rather than naming a winner.

expectedPoints.label: WebSocket = bidirectional
expectedPoints.description: WebSocket suits two-way, high-frequency communication like chat.

expectedPoints.label: SSE = one-way server push with auto-reconnect
expectedPoints.description: SSE streams server-to-client over HTTP and reconnects automatically; polling is simplest for low frequency.

followUps.prompt: What breaks first when you scale WebSocket connections, and how would you handle reconnection on the client?

questions.title: CSS box-sizing
questions.prompt: What does box-sizing do, and what is the difference between content-box and border-box?
questions.answerStructureHint: How width is computed with padding and border under each value, and why border-box is common.

expectedPoints.label: border-box includes padding and border in width
expectedPoints.description: With border-box the declared width contains content, padding and border.
acceptedSignals: padding and border inside the width

expectedPoints.label: content-box is the default and adds padding/border on top
expectedPoints.description: content-box (default) sizes only the content, so padding and border expand the element.

followUps.prompt: Why do many CSS resets set box-sizing: border-box on everything?

questions.title: Web accessibility basics
questions.prompt: How do you make a web application accessible? Walk me through the key things you check.
questions.answerStructureHint: Semantic HTML first, keyboard and focus, ARIA where needed, contrast and screen-reader testing.

expectedPoints.label: Semantic HTML first
expectedPoints.description: Use native elements (button, nav, label) before reaching for ARIA.

expectedPoints.label: Keyboard and focus management
expectedPoints.description: Everything usable by keyboard, visible focus, sensible tab order.

expectedPoints.label: ARIA where needed and sufficient contrast
expectedPoints.description: ARIA roles/attributes for custom widgets, plus color contrast and screen-reader checks.

followUps.prompt: How would you actually test that a component is accessible?

questions.title: Core Web Vitals
questions.prompt: What are the Core Web Vitals, and how would you improve a page that scores poorly on them?
questions.answerStructureHint: Name LCP/CLS/INP, measure with real data, then targeted fixes per metric.

expectedPoints.label: Measures with real data first
expectedPoints.description: Uses field data / Lighthouse / RUM before optimizing.

expectedPoints.label: Targeted fixes per metric
expectedPoints.description: Optimize images/critical path for LCP, reserve space for CLS, break up long tasks for INP.

followUps.prompt: A page jumps around as it loads. Which metric is that, and what are the usual causes?

questions.title: JavaScript event loop
questions.prompt: Explain the JavaScript event loop. What is the difference between a microtask and a macrotask?
questions.answerStructureHint: Single thread + call stack, the task and microtask queues, and their ordering.

expectedPoints.label: Single-threaded with a call stack and queue
expectedPoints.description: JS runs on one thread; the event loop pulls queued callbacks when the stack is empty.

expectedPoints.label: Microtasks drain before the next macrotask
expectedPoints.description: Promise callbacks (microtasks) run fully before the next timer/task (macrotask).

followUps.prompt: Given a console.log, a setTimeout(…, 0) and a Promise.then in the same function, in what order do they run and why?
